Title: HDR, the last enzyme in the MEP pathway, differently regulates isoprenoid biosynthesis in two woody plants

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PcIDI expression was significantly increased in highly PcHDR2-silenced poplar lines, suggesting compensatory adjustment of DMADP/IDP interconversion rather than a directly engineered causal determinant of terminal metabolite accumulation.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PcIDI transcripts increased in most strongly PcHDR2-silenced lines. 2. This change accompanied reduced DMADP and IDP pools. 3. Paired with unchanged DMADP:IDP ratio, the result suggests homeostatic buffering of precursor balance.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in transgenic PcHDR2 RNAi lines; metabolite profiling of DMADP and IDP
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in most highly PcHDR2-silenced lines; unchanged in less-silenced lines
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was experimentally measured in Results as part of metabolic compensation; however it is not the principal focal gene of the paper.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: DMADP and IDP are universal C5 precursors for isoprenoid biosynthesis; isoprene is associated with stress signaling and abiotic stress protection in plants.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-Seq data from previously obtained Populus trichocarpa herbivory- and pathogen-treatment transcriptomes; accession NA

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PaIDI expression was not altered by PaHDR1 or PaHDR2 silencing.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PaIDI transcript levels remained unchanged in PaHDR1 and PaHDR2 RNAi lines. 2. This indicates that spruce did not transcriptionally compensate HDR perturbation via IDI under the tested conditions.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in transgenic spruce RNAi lines
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Measured as a compensatory factor, not a primary focal gene.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: DMADP and IDP are universal C5 precursors for isoprenoid biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: HDR/IspH family; [4Fe-4S] cluster enzyme with plastid transit peptide
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal step of the plastidial MEP pathway supplying general isoprenoid precursor pools
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces and dehydroxylates HMBDP to form DMADP and IDP.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for precursor pool size; not established as the main rate-limiting step of the MEP pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: isoprene; monoterpenes; sesquiterpenes; carotenoids; chlorophyll side chains
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic pigments; volatile and defensive terpenoids; isoprene associated with abiotic stress protection/signaling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PcHDR2 is the dominant HDR isoform in poplar and is required for maintaining DMADP and IDP pools, MEP-pathway output, isoprene emission, prenyl diphosphate intermediates, carotenoids, chlorophylls, and normal growth.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PcHDR2 is expressed >100-fold higher than PcHDR1 in leaf, stem, and root, making it the dominant HDR isoform in poplar. 2. Recombinant PcHDR2 converts HMBDP to DMADP and IDP with a DMADP:IDP ratio of 1:9. 3. Strong RNAi silencing caused HMBDP accumulation, DMADP and IDP depletion, reduced GDP/FDP/GGDP and declines in isoprene, carotenoids, chlorophylls and some terpenoids, plus growth defects. 4. Overexpression up to about 2-fold did not significantly elevate downstream metabolites, indicating homeostatic buffering. 5. Silencing triggered compensatory upregulation of PcDXS2 and increased MEP-pathway flux in some lines.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; transgenic overexpression and RNAi; recombinant enzyme assay; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; volatile analysis; DXS activity assay; 13CO2 flux analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in leaf, stem, and root; dominant over PcHDR1 in all organs; PtHDR2 ortholog induced by herbivory, jasmonic acid, and root pathogen treatment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High constitutive expression in all organs
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Jasmonic acid treatment; herbivory; Phytophthora cactorum infection; greenhouse growth
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: maize ubiquitin1 promoter (constitutive) for overexpression and RNAi constructs
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong silencing caused ~50% reduced height, ~75% reduced leaf area, bleached leaves, delayed development; overexpression gave no gain in product levels
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful engineering target for altering plastidial isoprenoid precursor supply, but simple overexpression is ineffective and strong suppression causes severe growth and pigment penalties; likely requires coordinated stacking with downstream sink enzymes and flux-balancing factors.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Dominant poplar isoform despite presence of PcHDR1; high-DMADP product bias may contribute to excess DMADP disposal through isoprene emission.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-Seq in Populus trichocarpa stress datasets (accession NA)

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: HDR/IspH family; [4Fe-4S] cluster enzyme with plastid transit peptide
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal step of the plastidial MEP pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces and dehydroxylates HMBDP to form DMADP and IDP.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PcHDR1 is catalytically active but expressed at much lower levels than PcHDR2, implying a minor contribution to total poplar isoprenoid biosynthesis under tested conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PcHDR1 encodes an active HDR enzyme with higher catalytic efficiency than PcHDR2 in vitro. 2. PcHDR1 is expressed far below PcHDR2 in all tested organs. 3. PcHDR1 transcript levels were not altered by PcHDR2 overexpression or silencing. 4. Because of low expression, PcHDR1 likely contributes little to total poplar precursor formation in the tested tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cloning and sequencing; recombinant enzyme assay; RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Low in leaf, stem, and root; relatively more represented in roots than in aerial organs but still much lower than PcHDR2
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: JA treatment in P. trichocarpa increased ortholog transcripts; herbivory/pathogen mainly affected HDR2 ortholog
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential alternative HDR isoform for engineering because of high catalytic efficiency, but low native expression means its in planta contribution remains unresolved.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly functionally perturbed in transgenic poplar in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-Seq in Populus trichocarpa stress datasets (accession NA)

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: HDR/IspH family; [4Fe-4S] cluster enzyme with plastid transit peptide
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal step of the plastidial MEP pathway, primary-isoprenoid associated module
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces HMBDP to DMADP and IDP, with strong bias toward IDP production.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key determinant of DMADP:IDP composition in spruce primary metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: carotenoids; chlorophyll side chains; primary isoprenoids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic pigments and other primary plastidial isoprenoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PaHDR1 preferentially supplies IDP in spruce and is associated with primary isoprenoid metabolism in needles.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PaHDR1 is an active HDR enzyme with the strongest IDP bias among the tested HDRs (DMADP:IDP = 1:21). 2. PaHDR1 is highly expressed constitutively in needles and weakly in stems and roots. 3. RNAi silencing specifically reduced IDP but not DMADP, increasing the DMADP:IDP ratio. 4. These data support a role in supplying precursor balance for GGDP-derived primary products such as carotenoids and chlorophylls.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; recombinant enzyme assay; RNAi knock-down; LC-MS/MS profiling of DMADP and IDP
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutive high expression in needles; low in stems and roots; MJ induction only in roots
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pCAMBIA 1305.2 RNAi construct promoter not explicitly stated for spruce in the text
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing changed IDP pool and DMADP:IDP ratio but caused no evident growth or pigment phenotype, suggesting compensation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to tune IDP supply for GGDP-derived primary isoprenoids in conifers, though redundancy with PaHDR2 may buffer phenotypic effects.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Sequence identical to Picea glauca HDR1 and to Picea sitchensis PsHDR1 discussed in comparison.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: HDR/IspH family; [4Fe-4S] cluster enzyme with plastid transit peptide
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal step of the plastidial MEP pathway, specialized-terpenoid associated module
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces HMBDP to DMADP and IDP with relatively higher DMADP proportion than PaHDR1.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key determinant of precursor composition for specialized spruce terpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PaHDR2 is the dominant spruce HDR isoform in stems and roots, is MJ inducible, and likely supports specialized oleoresin terpenoid biosynthesis by producing relatively more DMADP than PaHDR1.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PaHDR2 encodes an active HDR enzyme with a DMADP:IDP ratio of 1:6, higher than PaHDR1. 2. PaHDR2 is dominant in stems and roots and induced by methyl jasmonate in all tissues tested. 3. Specific silencing had little effect on DMADP, IDP, prenyl diphosphates, or most terpenoid products, indicating strong compensation in spruce. 4. Expression pattern and product bias support a role in specialized oleoresin metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; methyl jasmonate induction; recombinant enzyme assay; RNAi knock-down; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Dominant in stems and roots; expressed in needles; induced by methyl jasmonate in all tested tissues, peaking at 2 d
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pCAMBIA 1305.2 RNAi construct promoter not explicitly stated for spruce in the text
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing had minimal phenotypic and metabolic effects, implying redundancy/homeostatic compensation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for conifer defense terpene engineering, but single-gene suppression appears insufficient because of pathway and paralog compensation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Inferred specialized role is based on expression domain, inducibility, and DMADP-biased product ratio rather than strong loss-of-function metabolite phenotypes.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional and enzymatic upregulation in compensatory feedback response
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MEP pathway entry step producing DXP
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Increased expression and enzyme activity in PcHDR2-silenced lines, associated with increased pathway flux
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Reduced DMADP/IDP pools after PcHDR2 silencing; possible feedback relief from end products
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Feedback/homeostatic response to lowered HDR products
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Reported as the main regulator and rate-limiting step of the MEP pathway; compensator of reduced HDR activity
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: plastidial isoprenoids including isoprene, carotenoids, chlorophyll side chains, mono-/sesquiterpenes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PcDXS2 was upregulated in PcHDR2-silenced lines and likely helped maintain/increase MEP pathway flux despite downstream blockage.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PcDXS2 transcripts increased in most strongly PcHDR2-silenced lines. 2. DXS enzyme activity also increased. 3. MEP pathway flux rose in two of three silenced lines, indicating homeostatic compensation. 4. The paper cites DXS as the major regulatory step of the pathway.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; DXS enzyme assay; 13CO2-label flux analysis
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Despite compensation, strong PcHDR2 silencing still caused major growth and pigment defects
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important co-engineering target with HDR for increasing plastidial terpenoid flux, but likely requires balancing downstream sink capacity.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not a transcription factor, it functions here as a decisive metabolic regulator discussed and experimentally assayed in Results.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: terminal branch enzyme controlling precursor drain
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: metabolic branch conversion of DMADP to isoprene
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: branch selection/removal of excess DMADP as isoprene emission
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: PcHDR2 perturbation altered PcIS transcript levels
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Proposed homeostatic safety-valve role for excess DMADP
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Converts DMADP directly to isoprene; discussed as possible outlet for excess DMADP
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stress protection/signaling; possible metabolic safety valve to remove excess DMADP
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PcIS transcript abundance increased in most PcHDR2 overexpression and RNAi lines, but isoprene emission tracked precursor availability rather than PcIS transcript level.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PcIS expression was significantly upregulated in most transgenic PcHDR2 lines. 2. Isoprene emission decreased in PcHDR2-silenced lines despite elevated PcIS transcripts. 3. Therefore, under these conditions isoprene formation was limited more by substrate supply than by PcIS transcript abundance.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; volatile collection and GC-MS analysis of isoprene emission
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in most PcHDR2 OE and RNAi lines
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be paired with HDR manipulation to redirect excess DMADP into volatile emission, though transcript upregulation alone is insufficient without precursor supply.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified here as regulatory/common branch-control factor rather than pathway core of the paper; principal study focus remains HDR isoforms.
